(1 comment)

Only issues are doxygen comment issue, and missing substitution pointed out by HPDD Checkpatch. Otherwise looks good.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

Fixed the doxygen issue. The HPDD check is actually wrong for hlist handling. If you look at libcfs/include/libcfs/list.h you will see that in newer kernel the number of args for hlist_for_each_X has changed. So we do need that wrapper to
handle current and newer kernels.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

There's still the missing conversion that HPDD Checkpatch pointed out.  Other than that it looks good.

Patch Set 6:

That HPDD check is not valid. hlist_for_each_entry_safe changed the number of arguments in newer kernels.

Patch Set 6:

I think the Checkpatch complaint about cfs_hlist_for_each_entry_safe() is incorrect.  That can't be directly replaced with the linux hlist equivalent as it's conditional based on autoconf results. The cfs_ wrapper is needed to adapt to different kernel primitives.
Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 6:

Correct. In fact their is a patch to deal with this at http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/11005

parents.subject: LU-3963 libcfs: remove invalid cfs_hlist_for_each_* checks

commit.subject: LU-3963 libcfs: convert lod, mdt, and gss to linux list api
commit.message:
LU-3963 libcfs: convert lod, mdt, and gss to linux list api

Move from the cfs_[h]list api to the native linux api for
the lod, mdt, and gss part of the ptlrpc layers.

Change-Id: Ieff231f3220a850521713a6f1c997b7e09130a4c
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/10387
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: frank zago <fzago@cray.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
